name: Hess's Law Thermochemical Cycle
description: Because enthalpy is a state function, a reaction enthalpy can be obtained indirectly via Hess's law: if standard formation enthalpies are available they are combined directly, otherwise a thermochemical cycle of known reactions is built and scaled, summed, and cross-checked against bond enthalpies to give the reaction enthalpy.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Target reaction ΔH?"]
    N2["Find known reactions"]
    N3{"Have ΔHf data?"}
    N4["Use formation enthalpies"]
    N5["Build thermochemical cycle"]
    N6["Scale / reverse steps"]
    N7["Sum via Hess's law"]
    N8["State-function check"]
    N9["Cross-check bond enthalpies"]
    N10["Reaction enthalpy ΔHrxn"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 -->|yes| N4
    N4 --> N7
    N3 -->|no| N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
